repository.users module¶

Users Repository Module¶

This module handles database operations for the User model, including registration, email confirmation, and profile updates.

repository.users.confirmed_email(email: str, db: Session) → None[source]¶

Marks a user’s email as verified in the database.

Parameters:

  • email (str) – The email address of the user who confirmed their registration.

  • db (Session) – The active database session.

Returns:

None

repository.users.create_user(body: UserSchema, db: Session) → User[source]¶

Creates a new user record in the database.

Parameters:

  • body (UserSchema) – The validated user data (from UserSchema).

  • db (Session) – The active database session.

Returns:

The newly created User object with its assigned ID.

Return type:

User

repository.users.get_user_by_email(email: str, db: Session) → User[source]¶

Retrieves a user from the database based on their unique email address.

Parameters:

  • email (str) – The email address to search for.

  • db (Session) – The active database session.

Returns:

The User object if found, otherwise None.

Return type:

User | None

repository.users.update_avatar(email: str, url: str, db: Session) → User[source]¶

Updates the avatar image URL for a specific user.

Parameters:

  • email (str) – The email of the user to update.

  • url (str) – The public URL of the uploaded avatar (e.g., from Cloudinary).

  • db (Session) – The active database session.

Returns:

The updated User object.

Return type:

User

repository.users.update_password(email: str, hashed_password: str, db: Session)[source]¶

Updates a user’s password in the database.

This function is typically used during the “forgot password” flow.

Parameters:

  • email (str) – The email address identifying the user.

  • hashed_password (str) – The new pre-hashed password.

  • db (Session) – The active database session.

Returns:

The User object if the password was updated, otherwise None.

Return type:

User | None

repository.users.update_token(user: User, token: str | None, db: Session) → None[source]¶

Updates the refresh token stored in the database for a specific user.

Parameters:

  • user (User) – The user instance to update.

  • token (str | None) – The new refresh token string or None to clear it.

  • db (Session) – The active database session.

Returns:

None
